evidence_quotes.quote: to revoke access to games they have already sold to their customers

paragraphs.text: The Imitative is an excellent start but I, like many others, feel like it's important to add specific focus to protections preventing video games publishers to revoke access to games they have already sold to their customers This is in line with the rest of the proposal's intentions and would greatly benefit consumers and set a positive precedent for the rest of the world to follow The "Stop Killing Games" initiative's proposals would be a good template to base the requirements for publishers and developers as it features nuanced and fair demands that don't needlessly impact businesses Videogames are a unique medium that face more challenges to preservation compared to more passive forms of media while still having a considerable impact on our culture, it is imperative for us to do what we can to prevent this form of art to be lost to time for no real reason As a software developer I understand some of the changes required to comply with these hypothetical new requirements are not without challenges but feasible and don't impact the ability of publishers to "pull the plug" on projects that are no longer economically viable I wholeheartedly believe that a dialog with the industry can and should be established in order to reach a compromise that satisfies both parties as far as details are concerned, but a resolute requirement must be insisted upon
